summary: Phase 3 V2 Step 2 — Webhook signal handlers. Verified live dormant 503 behaviour on supervised backend (no secrets set) for Twitch/Kick/YouTube (GET+POST), confirmed /api/webhooks/status returns expected JSON shape with all three *_configured=false and last_webhook_signal_by_source dict + callback_urls dict. Ran new pytest suite tests/test_phase3_v2_step2_webhooks.py: 16/16 PASS covering Twitch challenge handshake, valid notification → signals collection write (mocked=false, ingress=webhook), signature mismatch 403, missing headers 403, replay dedup, Kick valid/mismatch/missing-sig, YouTube GET challenge + invalid GET 400, YouTube notification write, YouTube bad signature → 202 silent, and 503-when-secrets-unset for all three sources. Full backend regression: 98/98 PASS (test_game_scores, test_mittari_api, test_phase3_pipeline, test_phase3_review, test_phase3_smartico, test_phase3_v2_source_scheduler, test_phase3_v2_step1_registries, test_phase3_v2_step2_webhooks, test_phase3_v2_voyager_rotation; LLM-dependent test_phase3a_v2_content.py intentionally skipped per handoff). 6 public regression endpoints (/api/dial, /api/operators, /api/streamers, /api/sources/public, /api/signals/live, /api/signup/count) all return 200. Router correctly mounted under /api prefix (POST /api/webhooks/twitch reachable; /webhooks/twitch at root not exposed). TTL index `ttl_first_seen` confirmed present on webhook_message_ids collection with expireAfterSeconds=600.

verified_features.live_status_endpoint: PASS — GET /api/webhooks/status returns 200 with twitch_configured/kick_configured/youtube_configured all false, last_webhook_signal_by_source populated from prior test signals, callback_urls dict all empty
verified_features.router_mount_prefix: PASS — all 4 webhook paths reachable under /api/webhooks/* via Kubernetes ingress; status endpoint confirms api_router.include_router(build_webhook_router(db)) mount is correct
verified_features.ttl_replay_index: PASS — webhook_message_ids collection has ttl_first_seen index with expireAfterSeconds=600 (created on startup via _ensure_replay_index)
verified_features.pytest_step2_webhooks_suite: PASS — 16/16 tests green covering all HMAC verification paths, challenge handshakes, replay dedup, dormant 503
verified_features.full_backend_regression: PASS — 98/98 pytest green (excluding LLM-gated test_phase3a_v2_content.py per handoff)

critical_code_review_comments: webhooks.py is well-scoped (~350 lines, single responsibility) with clean separation of secret-getter helpers, verifiers, replay protection, signal write, and router factory. Lazy env reads (called per-request) enable runtime config flips without restart and make per-test monkeypatching trivial.
Twitch verification correctly enforces ±600s timestamp skew window + HMAC-SHA256 over (msg_id+ts+body) — matches Twitch EventSub 2024+ spec exactly.

Replay protection via webhook_message_ids with Mongo TTL (600s) + DuplicateKeyError catch is idempotent and race-safe. YouTube uses sha256 of body as synthetic msg_id (no native id in Atom) — pragmatic choice.
YouTube PubSub bad-signature returns 202 (silent ack) per WebSub spec rather than 403 — correct behaviour; prevents subscription churn from hub misroutes.
Kick verification header is env-configurable (KICK_SIGNATURE_HEADER) — good forward-compatibility hedge given Kick's still-evolving public webhook docs.
Signal docs always carry ingress='webhook' + mocked=false — consistent with editorial honesty contract and downstream dial/aggregation pipeline shape from signal_engine.py polling adapters.

Minor (non-blocking): _record_message_id silently returns True when message_id is empty string — fine for YouTube path (always synthesises one) but means a Kick payload arriving with no Kick-Event-Message-Id header bypasses replay dedup. Consider logging a debug when this happens, or computing body-hash fallback like YouTube does.
test_credentials: X-Admin-Token: mittari-admin (not used for webhook endpoints — public, signed)
seed_data_creation: pytest suite writes Twitch/Kick/YouTube signals to MongoDB signals collection during TestClient runs (TEST_-style unique broadcaster logins via uuid4). Not cleaned up post-test but these are dev signals — webhook_message_ids entries auto-expire via TTL in 600s.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 V2 Step 2 (webhook signal handlers) verified end-to-end. Live supervised backend behaves correctly as dormant (503) on all webhook POST/GET because TWITCH_EVENTSUB_SECRET / KICK_WEBHOOK_SECRET / YOUTUBE_PUBSUB_SECRET are unset in /app/backend/.env — when those env vars are added, handlers will start processing real signed payloads. TTL index ttl_first_seen exists. All HMAC paths (SHA256 for Twitch+Kick, SHA1 for YouTube), replay dedup, challenge handshakes, and signature-mismatch responses verified via TestClient in tests/test_phase3_v2_step2_webhooks.py. Full backend regression 98/98 green. Frontend untouched this iteration (backend-only request).
